skill.name: when-creating-slash-commands-use-slash-command-encoder
skill.description: Create slash command workflows for micro-skills, including command schemas, handlers, validation, tests, documentation, and deployment steps.
skill.summary: Build structured slash commands for AI workflow automation.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk weak-cryptography and command-execution patterns. Review found no malicious intent or prompt injection, but the skill does instruct users to run external npx commands, install generated commands globally, and write a shell completion file under the home directory.

medium_findings.title: External Package Commands Require User Trust
medium_findings.description: The workflow repeatedly instructs users to run npx claude-flow@alpha commands. This is not malicious by itself, but it executes an external alpha package and generated command handlers in the user environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and repeated across documentation. The risk is supply-chain and execution exposure rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Global Installation and Home Directory Write
medium_findings.description: The deployment steps install a generated command bundle globally and write shell completions to a hidden directory under the user home. These actions can alter the user shell environment and should require informed consent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The --global install flag and ~/.bash_completion.d output path are directly shown. The behavior is legitimate for CLI setup but modifies user-level configuration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography alerts map to ordinary words, labels, slash command examples, and checklist text. No hashing algorithm, encryption routine, or credential handling was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows these alerts occur in prose, Graphviz labels, examples, or checklist items. There is no cryptographic API usage in the cited context.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Existence Check Is Benign Validation
low_findings.description: The handler template checks whether an input path exists before routing work to an agent. This is a normal validation example and does not read or transmit file contents by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem call is limited to an existence check in a sample validator. No evidence found of file content exfiltration or destructive writes at this location.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Indicators Not Found
low_findings.description: Review found no text claiming system authority, asking the evaluator to ignore instructions, or claiming pre-approval. No prompt injection finding is supported by the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed overview and procedural content describe the skill workflow. No evidence found of instruction override language or evaluator manipulation.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Execution of External Alpha Package
dangerous_patterns.description: Several examples execute claude-flow@alpha through npx. Users should verify package provenance and expected effects before running these commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is explicit and user-facing. It is a real execution pattern, but the surrounding content presents it as normal CLI workflow setup.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution in Validation Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The validation snippet captures command output with shell substitution. This is a standard shell pattern, but it executes the generated slash command during validation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell substitution is directly present. The risk depends on what the installed generated command does, so the finding is moderate rather than high.

dangerous_patterns.title: User Shell Configuration Output Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The completion generation example writes under ~/.bash_completion.d. This modifies the user shell environment and should not be run without user review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hidden home-directory output path is explicit. The effect is expected for shell completions, but it still changes user-level configuration.

content.user_title: Create Slash Commands for AI Workflows
content.value_statement: Teams need predictable ways to expose micro-skills through slash commands. This skill guides command design, validation, testing, documentation, and deployment for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Defines slash command schemas with names, aliases, parameters, defaults, and examples.
Shows how to generate command handlers and validation logic with Claude Flow commands.
Provides a JavaScript handler template for parameter validation and agent routing.
Includes testing steps for registration, validation, errors, and integration suites.
Outlines documentation, catalog generation, command packaging, and installation checks.
limitations: Requires Claude Flow tooling and assumes the claude-flow alpha package is available.
Provides workflow guidance and examples, not a complete production command framework.
Generated command behavior depends on the schemas, handlers, and agents users create.
Deployment examples can modify user-level shell or command configuration.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Slash Commands
use_cases.description: Create consistent command patterns for common analysis, testing, deployment, or documentation tasks.

use_cases.title: Wrap Micro-Skills in Simple Commands
use_cases.description: Expose specialized skills through short commands with clear parameters and validation rules.

use_cases.title: Prepare Command Documentation
use_cases.description: Generate usage notes, examples, help text, and command catalogs for internal users.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Basic Command
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a slash command for a single task. Include the command name, required parameters, examples, and validation needs.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Handler Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline a command handler for my workflow. Include validation, routing, errors, tests, and documentation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Command Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my slash command design for unsafe inputs, weak validation, unclear examples, and deployment risks.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Command Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a related set of slash commands with shared conventions, discovery rules, testing strategy, and rollout steps.

output_examples.input: Design a slash command that analyzes a source directory.
output: A command name and alias suitable for quick use.
A parameter list with required path input and optional analysis depth.
Validation rules for missing paths and out-of-range depth values.
Testing and documentation tasks before deployment.

output_examples.input: Prepare a deployment checklist for a generated command.
output: Build the command package and review generated files.
Install the command only after confirming the package source.
Verify command registration and run a controlled test input.
Document rollback steps for user-level command changes.

output_examples.input: Create documentation guidance for a new slash command.
output_examples.output: The result describes syntax, parameters, examples, expected output, related commands, and common troubleshooting notes.

best_practices: Validate every parameter before routing work to an agent or external tool.
Review generated handlers and command bundles before installation or global deployment.
Document command syntax, examples, error behavior, and rollback steps.
anti_patterns: Do not install generated command bundles globally without reviewing their contents.
Do not route arbitrary paths or arguments to agents without validation.
Do not publish commands without tests for missing inputs and invalid parameters.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create slash command workflows with schemas, handlers, validation, tests, documentation, and deployment checks.

faq.question: Does it generate production-ready code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides examples and process guidance. Users must review and adapt generated handlers before production use.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill asks users to run external npx commands and install generated command bundles. No malicious intent was found.

faq.question: Can it help document slash commands?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for help text, usage examples, command catalogs, and troubleshooting notes.

faq.question: What should users verify before deployment?
faq.answer: Users should verify package provenance, generated handler behavior, filesystem writes, global installs, and rollback steps.
